text: When you write code, you will inevitably encounter bugs—errors that stop your program from working correctly. The first step is identifying what is wrong. Start by reading error messages carefully; they often tell you exactly where the problem is. Next, use debugging tools like breakpoints in your development environment. These let you pause the program and inspect variable values step by step. A systematic approach is key: change only one thing at a time and test immediately. Finally, add logging to your code. Printing messages to the console helps you track what your program is doing and where it goes wrong. With practice, these strategies make debugging much less frustrating.
